channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Foreign aid freeze temporarily lifted but damage may already be done
item.description: Some Republican lawmakers introduced legislation to find a way to work around Trump administration cuts to USAID.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge delays Labor Department's request to block DOGE access
item.description: A federal judge has indefinitely delayed a ruling on whether the Labor Department's request to block billionaire Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing internal system data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy will kick off his bid for Ohio governor in Cincinnati on Feb. 24
item.description: Republican Vivek Ramaswamy will launch his bid for Ohio governor on Feb. 24 in his native Cincinnati, with ensuing events in the Columbus, Toledo and Cleveland areas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith (D) will not run for reelection in 2026, opening up a seat in the Democratic-leaning state, she announced Thursday. Smith said in a video posted to the social platform X that she has “loved” her job as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Vance jokes about Greta Thunberg as he goes scorched earth on European censorship
item.description: American Vice President JD Vance joked about Greta Thunberg's scolding criticism of the U.S. in a speech that roasted European censorship at the Munich Security Conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Trump official teases 2026 bid for California governor if Harris jumps in race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a longtime Donald Trump loyalist who is serving as U.S. envoy for special missions in the president's second administration, is floating a potential bid for California governor if former Vice President Kamala Harris also runs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Saudi Arabia's crown prince became a central player in US-brokered peace talks between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: Saudi Arabia has become a central player in U.S. negotiations for a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine because of the crucial role oil exports play in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — There was guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi, including F-35 stealth fighter jets, to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Majority Leader Thune says this is the reason why he and Trump are working well together
item.description: GOP Senate Majority Leader John Thune talks exclusively with Fox News Digital about dealing with President Donald Trump and his working relationship with former leader Mitch McConnell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Can suspending a cage-free egg law solve the soaring price problem? Nevada takes a crack at it
item.description: Record high egg prices prompted Nevada lawmakers to unanimously back suspending their cage-free egg law, but the six other states with similar laws in effect have shown no sign of dropping them even if it might help lower grocery bills.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Sejal Govindarao And Ty Oneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: White House blocks AP reporter from Trump-Modi news conference because of Gulf of Mexico fight
item.description: The White House blocked an Associated Press journalist from covering a news conference with two major world leaders Thursday, upping the stakes in a disagreement between the news agency and the Trump administration over AP's style decision to stick with the name “Gulf of Mexico” for the body of wate
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Sen. Tina Smith’s retirement means for Minnesota politics in 2026
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith announced she wouldn’t seek reelection on Thursday, opening a big field on both sides to replace her in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Alanna Elder, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Minnesotans, other politicians react to Sen. Tina Smith's decision not to run again
item.description: Minnesota politicians from both sides of the aisle have weighed in on the news that U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, who has served in the Senate since 2018, is retiring after next year.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump funding freeze could leave communities on their own as climate threats grow
item.description: Without federal support, American communities will struggle to deal with a challenge as pervasive as climate change, market analysts and environmental advocates say.
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE takes aim at agency that had plans of regulating X
item.description: Musk and other Silicon Valley executives have long railed against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for its work overseeing the tech industry. Now, Musk hopes to eliminate the agency.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical field needs more training like cultural competency, not less, Nevada lawmakers argue
item.description: A proposal to encourage — not require — medical practitioners to get training in subjects ranging from suicide prevention to cultural competency drew an extremely cold reaction from lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
